text: Three persons are a man's supreme elders (*atiguru*). // Vi_31.1 //
The mother, the father, and the teacher. // Vi_31.2 //
One must always be ready to serve them. // Vi_31.3 //
What they say, that one shall do. // Vi_31.4 //
One shall do what is pleasing and beneficial to them. // Vi_31.5 //
Without their permission, one shall not do anything at all. // Vi_31.6 //

> These three are the three Vedas, these three are the three gods, /
> These three are the three worlds, these three are the three fires. || Vi_31.7 ||
The father is the Gārhapatya fire, the mother is the Dakṣiṇāgni, and the teacher is the Āhavanīya. // Vi_31.8 //

> All his religious duties are honored, for whom these three are honored; /
> But for him by whom these are dishonored, all his rites are fruitless. || Vi_31.9 ||
>
> Through devotion to his mother, he gains this world; through devotion to his father, the middle world; /
> But through service to his teacher, he attains the world of Brahma. || Vi_31.10 ||
